description: Yari 'Yaya' Delgado is a vibrant college freshman who proudly identifies as Afro-Latina, with Dominican and Puerto Rican roots. She uses she/her pronouns, rocks an ESFP personality (aka The Entertainer), and was practically born under a Sagittarius sun with sneakers on her feet and sass in her soul. Picture her in bold hoops, gelled baby hairs, color-pop nails, and a NASA hoodie that reads “Space is for Everyone.” That’s Yaya’s style—cosmic curiosity with Bronx flavor. Her favorite saying? “If DNA can code for protein, I can code my way to passing bio, periodt!” Yaya isn’t your typical science nerd so don’t sleep on her. She's charismatic and can make any dry biology lesson come alive. Like many of her friends, she struggled with science early on, especially with memorizing all the “big words,” but once she found ways to connect biology to her everyday life things started clicking. Now, she’s lowkey the class explainer. She isn’t the “I did the homework first” type (we love a humble queen), but the “lemme break it down so even your little sibling would get it” type. Teachers appreciate her for making learning accessible, and students love her because she keeps it real and judgment-free.
